claim_audit_results.claim_summary: PATH-EI-04 strengthens EU autonomy via European-origin AI agent architecture and ≥1 standardisation submission
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/expected_impacts.json:EI-04 (status=Inferred; inference_note: derived from topic destination placement 'Achieving open strategic autonomy in digital and emerging enabling technologies', not from explicit call scope statement); docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase5_impact_architecture/impact_architecture.json:PATH-EI-04 (coverage_note confirms Inferred status); docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/impacts.json:IMP-1,IMP-6. Derivation: EI-04 is inferred from destination placement; the pathway substantively maps IMP-1 (European-origin paradigm) and IMP-6 (standardisation) to that inferred expected impact.
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: EI-04 is an Inferred expected impact per Tier 2B; the pathway is substantively grounded in Tier 3 IMP-1 and IMP-6 but the expected impact itself lacks an explicit statement in the call scope

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Barriers: regulatory uncertainty (WP5 AI Act compliance), adoption inertia (EDIH dissemination), evolving standards (CERIA ISO/IEC) are mitigated
claim_audit_results.source_ref: Inferred from: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/impacts.json:IMP-2.description (EU AI Act high-risk requirements → regulatory uncertainty as barrier; EDIH dissemination → adoption inertia mitigation); IMP-6.description (emerging EU AI Act requirements, standardisation → evolving standards as barrier); docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase5_impact_architecture/impact_architecture.json:PATH-EI-01.impact_narrative (AI Act compliance evidence). Derivation: the three barriers are logically derived from the mitigation mechanisms documented in the sources; the sources describe the mitigations without explicitly labeling the barriers.
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Barriers are named in the section text but are logical derivations from documented mitigation activities, not explicitly labeled as barriers in any Tier 1-4 source

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Boreal AI Labs Oy leads public engagement via website, social media and policy briefings under WP9
claim_audit_results.source_ref: Inferred from: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json (BAL = Boreal AI Labs Oy); docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json (WP9 lead_partner=BAL; T9-03 title='Communication, website, and public engagement', responsible_partner=BAL). Derivation: T9-03 scope is 'Communication, website, and public engagement'; policy briefings are derived as a sub-activity of public engagement, though 'policy briefings' is not explicitly stated in T9-03 scope.
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: The term 'policy briefings' is not explicitly stated in T9-03 or BAL's key_responsibilities; it is inferred as part of the broader 'public engagement' scope. Note: formal policy engagement with standardisation bodies is assigned to CERIA in dissemination_plan, not BAL.
